asan.evidence_summary: ASan reproduced a real libupb abort in upb_FieldPathVector_Reserve() after truncated allocation arithmetic requested 0xffffffff80000000 bytes.

lldb.evidence_summary: LLDB launch failed, but the required printf fallback captured oldsize/newsize narrowing from 0x80000000 and 0x100000000 down to negative and zero int values before the same reserve-path abort.

fresh.evidence_summary: Independent review found the same size_t-to-int truncation in required_fields.c and noted unchecked capacity doubling in the reserve helper.

impact.evidence_summary: The issue is reachable through exported upb and Python protobuf APIs and was demonstrated as a denial-of-service style abort while collecting missing required-field paths.

notes: The two blind runtime validators agree on the same reserve-path integer truncation bug: ASan shows the crash manifestation and LLDB fallback captured the bad arithmetic that produces it. Fresh validation independently identified the same source-level defect. Impact validation narrows the consequence to a demonstrated DoS-style abort rather than a proven arbitrary heap overwrite, but it does not contradict the underlying bug.
